data.question: What is the most expensive cake and its flavor?

prediction.sql: SELECT Flavor, Price FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Food) = LOWER('cake') AND Price = (SELECT MAX(Price) FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Food) = LOWER('cake'))

data.question: What is the cheapest cookie and its flavor?

prediction.sql: SELECT Flavor, Price FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Food) = LOWER('cookie') ORDER BY Price ASC LIMIT 1

data.question: Find the ids of goods that have apple flavor.

prediction.sql: SELECT Id FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Flavor) = LOWER('apple');

data.question: What are the ids of goods that cost less than 3 dollars?

prediction.sql: SELECT Id FROM goods WHERE Price < 3

data.question: List the distinct ids of all customers who bought a lemon cake?

prediction.sql: SELECT DISTINCT r.CustomerId FROM receipts r JOIN items i ON r.ReceiptNumber = i.Receipt JOIN goods g ON i.Item = g.Id WHERE LOWER(g.Flavor) = LOWER('lemon') AND LOWER(g.Food) = LOWER('cake')

data.question: For each type of food, tell me how many customers have ever bought it.

prediction.sql: SELECT g.Food, COUNT(DISTINCT r.CustomerId) FROM goods g LEFT JOIN items i ON g.Id = i.Item LEFT JOIN receipts r ON i.Receipt = r.ReceiptNumber GROUP BY g.Food

data.question: Find the id of customers who shopped at the bakery at least 15 times.

prediction.sql: SELECT CustomerId FROM receipts GROUP BY CustomerId HAVING COUNT(*) >= 15

data.question: What is the last name of the customers who shopped at the bakery more than 10 times?

prediction.sql: SELECT customers.LastName FROM customers INNER JOIN receipts ON customers.Id = receipts.CustomerId GROUP BY customers.Id HAVING COUNT(receipts.ReceiptNumber) > 10

data.question: How many types of Cake does this bakery sell?

prediction.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT Flavor) FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Food) = LOWER('Cake');

data.question: List all the flavors of Croissant available in this bakery.

prediction.sql: SELECT DISTINCT Flavor FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Food) = LOWER('Croissant');

data.question: Give me a list of all the distinct items bought by the customer number 15.

prediction.sql: SELECT DISTINCT items.Item FROM items INNER JOIN receipts ON items.Receipt = receipts.ReceiptNumber WHERE receipts.CustomerId = 15

data.question: For each type of food, what are the average, maximum and minimum price?

prediction.sql: SELECT Food, AVG(Price), MAX(Price), MIN(Price) FROM goods GROUP BY Food

data.question: Find the receipt numbers where both Cake and Cookie were bought.

prediction.sql: SELECT items.Receipt FROM items JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id WHERE goods.Food IN ('Cake', 'Cookie') GROUP BY items.Receipt HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT goods.Food) = 2;

data.question: Find all the receipt numbers in which customer id 5 purchased Croissant.
What is the receipt number and date of the receipt in which the most expensive item was bought?

prediction.sql: SELECT receipts.ReceiptNumber, receipts.Date FROM receipts JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id WHERE goods.Price = (SELECT MAX(Price) FROM goods) LIMIT 1

data.question: What is the item that was bought the least number of times?

prediction.sql: SELECT Item, COUNT(*) AS count FROM items GROUP BY Item ORDER BY count ASC LIMIT 1;

data.question: How many goods are available for each food type?

prediction.sql: SELECT Food, COUNT(Id) FROM goods GROUP BY Food;

data.question: What is the average price for each food type?

prediction.sql: SELECT Food, AVG(Price) FROM goods GROUP BY Food

data.question: What are the goods that have Apricot flavor and are cheaper than 5 dollars?

prediction.sql: SELECT * FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Flavor) = LOWER('Apricot') AND Price < 5

data.question: Find flavor of cakes that cost more than 10 dollars.

prediction.sql: SELECT Flavor FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Food) = LOWER('cake') AND Price > 10

data.question: Give me the distinct id and price for all goods whose price is below the average of all goods?

prediction.sql: SELECT DISTINCT Id, Price FROM goods WHERE Price < (SELECT AVG(Price) FROM goods)

data.question: What are the distinct ids of all goods that are cheaper than some goods of type Tart?

prediction.sql: SELECT DISTINCT g.Id FROM goods g WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM goods t WHERE LOWER(t.Food) = LOWER('Tart') AND g.Price < t.Price)

data.question: List distinct receipt numbers for which someone bought a good that costs more than 13 dollars.
On which date did some customer buy a good that costs more than 15 dollars?

prediction.sql: SELECT DISTINCT receipts.Date FROM receipts JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id WHERE goods.Price > 15

data.question: Give me the list of all goods whose id has "APP".

prediction.sql: SELECT * FROM goods WHERE Id LIKE '%APP%';

data.question: Which good has "70" in its id? And what is its price?

prediction.sql: SELECT Id, Price FROM goods WHERE Id LIKE '%70%';

data.question: List the last names of all customers in an alphabetical oder.

prediction.sql: SELECT LastName FROM customers ORDER BY LastName;

data.question: Return the ordered list of all good ids.

prediction.sql: SELECT Id FROM goods ORDER BY Id;

data.question: Find all receipts in which either apple flavor pie was bought or customer id 12 shopped.
Find all receipts which has the latest date. Also tell me that date.

prediction.sql: SELECT * FROM receipts WHERE Date = (SELECT MAX(Date) FROM receipts)

data.question: Find all receipts which either has the earliest date or has a good with price above 10.
What are the ids of Cookie and Cake that cost between 3 and 7 dollars.

prediction.sql: SELECT Id FROM goods WHERE Food IN ('Cookie', 'Cake') AND Price BETWEEN 3 AND 7

data.question: Find the first name and last name of a customer who visited on the earliest date.

prediction.sql: SELECT c.FirstName, c.LastName FROM customers c JOIN receipts r ON c.Id = r.CustomerId WHERE r.Date = (SELECT MIN(Date) FROM receipts)

data.question: What is average price of goods whose flavor is blackberry or blueberry?

prediction.sql: SELECT AVG(Price) FROM goods WHERE Flavor IN ('blackberry', 'blueberry')

data.question: Return the cheapest price for goods with cheese flavor.

prediction.sql: SELECT MIN(Price) FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Flavor) = LOWER('cheese');

data.question: What are highest, lowest, and average prices of goods, grouped and ordered by flavor?

prediction.sql: SELECT Flavor, MAX(Price) AS Highest, MIN(Price) AS Lowest, AVG(Price) AS Average FROM goods GROUP BY Flavor ORDER BY Flavor;

data.question: Return the lowest and highest prices of goods grouped and ordered by food type.

prediction.sql: SELECT Food, MIN(Price) AS lowest, MAX(Price) AS highest FROM goods GROUP BY Food ORDER BY Food

data.question: Find the top three dates when the most goods were sold.

prediction.sql: SELECT Date, COUNT(ReceiptNumber) AS count FROM receipts GROUP BY Date ORDER BY count DESC LIMIT 3

data.question: Which customer shopped most often? How many times?

prediction.sql: SELECT CustomerId, COUNT(*) AS shopping_count FROM receipts GROUP BY CustomerId ORDER BY shopping_count DESC LIMIT 1;

data.question: For each date, return how many distinct customers visited on that day.

prediction.sql: SELECT Date, COUNT(DISTINCT CustomerId) FROM receipts GROUP BY Date

data.question: Give me the first name and last name of customers who have bought apple flavor Tart.

prediction.sql: SELECT customers.FirstName, customers.LastName FROM customers JOIN receipts ON customers.Id = receipts.CustomerId JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id WHERE LOWER(goods.Flavor) = LOWER('apple') AND LOWER(goods.Food) = LOWER('Tart')

data.question: What are the ids of Cookies whose price is lower than any Croissant?

prediction.sql: SELECT Id FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Food) = LOWER('Cookie') AND Price < (SELECT MIN(Price) FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Food) = LOWER('Croissant'))

data.question: Give me the ids of Cakes whose price is at least three times as much as the average price of Tart?

prediction.sql: SELECT Id FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Food) = LOWER('Cake') AND Price >= 3 * (SELECT AVG(Price) FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Food) = LOWER('Tart'))

data.question: What are the ids of goods whose price is above twice the average price of all goods?

prediction.sql: SELECT Id FROM goods WHERE Price > 2 * (SELECT AVG(Price) FROM goods)

data.question: List the id, flavor and type of food of goods ordered by price.

prediction.sql: SELECT Id, Flavor, Food FROM goods ORDER BY Price;

data.question: Return a list of the id and flavor for Cakes ordered by flavor.

prediction.sql: SELECT Id, Flavor FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Food) = LOWER('Cake') ORDER BY Flavor

data.question: Find all the items that have chocolate flavor but were not bought more than 10 times.

prediction.sql: SELECT items.Item FROM items JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id WHERE LOWER(goods.Flavor) = LOWER('chocolate') GROUP BY items.Item HAVING COUNT(*) <= 10

data.question: What are the flavors available for Cake but not for Tart?

prediction.sql: SELECT Flavor FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Food) = LOWER('Cake') AND Flavor NOT IN (SELECT Flavor FROM goods WHERE LOWER(Food) = LOWER('Tart'))

data.question: What is the three most popular goods in this bakery?

prediction.sql: SELECT Item, COUNT(*) AS count FROM items GROUP BY Item ORDER BY count DESC LIMIT 3

data.question: Find the ids of customers who have spent more than 150 dollars in total.

prediction.sql: SELECT receipts.CustomerId FROM receipts JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id GROUP BY receipts.CustomerId HAVING SUM(goods.Price) > 150

data.question: Find the ids of customers whose average spending for each good is above 5.

prediction.sql: SELECT r.CustomerId FROM receipts r JOIN items i ON r.ReceiptNumber = i.Receipt JOIN goods g ON i.Item = g.Id GROUP BY r.CustomerId HAVING AVG(g.Price) > 5;

data.question: On which day did the bakery sell more than 100 dollars in total.

prediction.sql: SELECT receipts.Date FROM receipts JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id GROUP BY receipts.Date HAVING SUM(items.Ordinal * goods.Price) > 100;
